description: Polish-American organized crime refers to criminal groups composed of Polish immigrants and Polish Americans operating primarily in the United States. Active since the 1920s, these groups have been involved in bootlegging, drug trafficking, racketeering, and other illegal activities. Notable factions include the Kielbasa Posse in Philadelphia and the Greenpoint Crew in New York. They have historically allied with the Chicago Outfit and rivaled Irish and Russian mobs.

country_links.evidence_quote: Territory: Poland , Western and Central Europe , New York City , Philadelphia , Cleveland , Chicago , Orlando , New Jersey , Dallas , Omaha , Pittsburgh , Detroit , Buffalo , St. Louis
country_links.context: The organization has territory in Poland.
